title: Map bass response to macro motion
summary: Bass-to-macro-motion infrastructure is already extensively implemented across three levels: camera orbit modulation (cameraMotion.ts), mesh-level rotation (geometry systems), and per-vertex shader deformation (radial expansion, noise displacement, twist, drift). The remaining work is to unskip the three pending tests that validate displacement correlation, bounds safety, and reduced-motion respect, fix any issues they reveal, and verify all four acceptance criteria end-to-end.

currentState.audioExtraction: computeBassAvg averages first 25% of FFT bins (0-255), smoothed via EMA (0.88/0.12) in renderLoop.ts
currentState.mapping: bassToMacro in mappings.ts converts 0-255 raw to 0-1 via power curve (exponent 0.7)
currentState.cameraMotion: cameraMotion.ts scales orbit radius by 1 + bassEnergy * 0.15
currentState.meshMotion: Each geometry system applies bass-driven Y-rotation: rotation.y += bassEnergy * motionAmplitude * 0.1 * sin(t)
currentState.shaderDeformation: Vertex shaders apply four bass-driven effects: radial expansion (1 + uBassEnergy * 0.25), FBM noise displacement, twist around Y-axis, directional drift
currentState.syntheticFallback: When rawBass < 5, inject 30 + 25 * sin(elapsed * 0.001) to keep visuals alive while muted

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.criterion: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.evidence: Camera orbit radius, mesh rotation, and four per-vertex shader effects all driven by uBassEnergy
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.validation: Unskip T-018-01 to verify displacement correlates with bass energy

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.criterion: Response is smoothed to avoid jitter
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.evidence: EMA smoothing (factor 0.88) in renderLoop.ts, verified by passing T-018-02

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.criterion: Effect remains visible when audio is unmuted or muted
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.evidence: Synthetic fallback injects bass signal when rawBass < 5, verified by passing T-018-03

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.criterion: No clipping or visual instability occurs
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.evidence: Radial expansion capped at 1.25x, drift capped by sin/cos bounds, but T-018-04 is skipped
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.validation: Unskip T-018-04 to verify no out-of-bounds positions at max bass; unskip T-018-05 to verify reduced-motion compliance

steps.detail: Remove .skip from T-018-01. This test validates that higher bass energy produces larger vertex displacement. It was skipped during Canvas2D→Three.js migration. Update the test to work with the current Three.js geometry system — it needs to read positions from BufferGeometry after a draw() call with varying bassEnergy values and assert that displacement magnitude increases with bass energy.
steps.risk: Test may reference old Canvas2D APIs; will need to mock or instantiate the geometry system correctly

steps.detail: Remove .skip from T-018-04. This test verifies that at maximum bass (255), vertex positions remain within reasonable bounds and no NaN/Infinity values appear. Update to work with current shader uniform pipeline. If the test reveals positions escaping frustum, add clamping in the vertex shader's final position output.
steps.risk: May reveal edge cases where stacked deformations (expansion * noise * twist * drift) push vertices too far; fix by clamping final displacement magnitude

steps.detail: Remove .skip from T-018-05. This test verifies that when prefers-reduced-motion is active (uMotionAmplitude = 0.2), bass-driven displacement is proportionally reduced. All shader effects already multiply by `ma` (motionAmplitude), so this should pass once the test is updated for the current API.
steps.risk: Low — motionAmplitude scaling is already wired through all shader deformation paths

steps.action: Run full test suite and fix any failures
steps.detail: Run `npx vitest run` to execute all tests including the newly unskipped ones. Fix any test infrastructure issues (mocks, geometry instantiation, uniform access patterns) that arise from the Canvas2D→Three.js migration gap.
steps.risk: Test setup may need Three.js mocking or headless GL context

steps.detail: Run `npm run build` to confirm no TypeScript or bundling errors. Load in browser and verify: (a) bass visibly drives macro motion, (b) no console errors, (c) no WebGL validation errors, (d) muted mode still shows motion via synthetic fallback.
steps.risk: Low — no production code changes expected

steps.detail: Set passes=true in the ticket tracking once all tests pass and build succeeds.

productionCodeChanges: Likely none — all bass-to-macro-motion logic is already implemented. Changes limited to test fixes unless T-018-04 reveals a clamping gap in shaders.
risks: Skipped tests may have stale assertions referencing Canvas2D-era APIs
Headless GL context may be needed for GPU-side displacement verification
Stacked shader deformations at max bass could theoretically push vertices out of frustum (mitigated by existing per-effect caps)

dependencies.US-009: Audio analysis pipeline — already implemented and working
dependencies.US-017: Shader infrastructure — already implemented with all uniforms wired
